content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://www.massgeneralbrigham.org/en/about/newsroom/articles/pros-and-cons-of-intermittent-fasting", "title": "Intermittent Fasting Benefits, Risks, and How it Works", "summary": "Intermittent Fasting Benefits and Risks Food is fuel for your body. But getting the right balance of calories and nutrients can be tricky. Intermittent fasting is one approach to eating that\u2019s gained a lot of popularity.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["But is it a smart strategy, or a passing fad? \u201cIt\u2019s not a magic cure for losing weight, but the research on intermittent fasting is promising,\u201d says Mary Hyer, RD, LDN, CCRP.", "That depends. \u201cThere are lots of options to choose from,\u201d Hyer says. \u201cBut in the scientific world, you have to go at least 12 hours for it to be considered a fast.\u201d How does intermittent fasting work?", "Your body usually burns glucose (sugar) for energy, but after about 10 to 12 hours without food your glucose stores run low.", "Research\u2014mainly from animal and cellular studies\u2014suggests this housekeeping of damaged or injured cells may help reduce inflammation and prevent damage to healthy cells.", "While intermittent fasting isn\u2019t a prevention or cure for any condition, talk to your primary care provider about whether it may be beneficial for you."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: But is it a smart strategy, or a passing fad? \u201cIt\u2019s not a magic cure for losing weight, but the research on intermittent"}, {"url": "https://www.mayoclinic.org/healthy-lifestyle/nutrition-and-healthy-eating/expert-answers/intermittent-fasting/faq-20441303", "title": "Intermittent fasting: What are the benefits?", "summary": "Cookies help us give you the best experience. Necessary cookies are required for the site to work. Optional cookies help us improve the site and show you personalized ads.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Cookies help us give you the best experience.", "Necessary cookies are required for the site to work."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Cookies help us give you the best experience."}, {"url": "https://www.health.harvard.edu/healthy-aging-and-longevity/4-intermittent-fasting-side-effects-to-watch-out-for", "title": "4 intermittent fasting side effects to watch out for", "summary": "Coping with chronic pain, depression, and high blood pressure Can you prevent the hunched back of kyphosis? The idea is that it's easier to sharply restrict calories a few days a week or to limit eating to a shortened \"eating window\" each day than it is to moderately cut calories at every meal, every day. Proponents claim that extended fasting periods (beyond the normal time between meals) promote cellular repair, improve insulin sensitivity, increase levels of human growth hormone, and alter gene expression in a way that promotes longevity and disease protection.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The idea is that it's easier to sharply restrict calories a few days a week or to limit eating to a shortened \"eating window\" each day than it is to moderately cut calories at every meal, every day.", "To decrease some of these unwanted side effects, you may want to switch from adf fasting to periodic fasting or a time restricted eating plan that allows you to eat everyday within a certain time period. #2.", "It's human nature for people to want to reward themselves after doing very hard work, such as exercise or fasting for a long period of time, so there is a danger of indulging in unhealthy dietary habits on non-fasting days.", "While intermittent fasting shows promise, there is even less evidence about the benefits or how fasting might affect older adults.", "Human studies have looked mostly at small groups of young or middle-aged adults, for only short periods of time."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: The idea is that it's easier to sharply restrict calories a few days a week or to limit eating to a shortened \"eating wi"}, {"url": "https://newsroom.heart.org/news/8-hour-time-restricted-eating-linked-to-a-91-higher-risk-of-cardiovascular-death", "title": "8-hour time-restricted eating linked to a 91% higher risk of ...", "summary": "03/19/24 Editor\u2019s note: - The research authors have shared their full poster presentation for updated details about their research abstract. As noted in all American Heart Association scientific meetings news releases, research abstracts are considered preliminary until published in a peer-reviewed scientific journal. The meeting offers the latest science on population-based health and wellness and implications for lifestyle.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Time-restricted eating, a type of intermittent fasting, involves limiting the hours for eating to a specific number of hours each day, which may range from a 4- to 12-hour time window in 24 hours.", "Many people who follow a time-restricted eating diet follow a 16:8 eating schedule, where they eat all their foods in an 8-hour window and fast for the remaining 16 hours each day, the researchers noted.", "They reviewed information about dietary patterns for participants in the annual 2003-2018 National Health and Nutrition Examination Surveys (NHANES) in comparison to data about people who died in the U.S., from 2003 through December 2019, from the Ce", "The study\u2019s limitations included its reliance on self-reported dietary information, which may be affected by participant\u2019s memory or recall and may not accurately assess typical eating patterns.", "Statements and conclusions of studies that are presented at the American Heart Association\u2019s scientific meetings are solely those of the study authors and do not necessarily reflect the Association\u2019s policy or position."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Time-restricted eating, a type of intermittent fasting, involves limiting the hours for eating to a specific number of h"}, {"url": "https://www.npjournal.org/article/S1555-4155%252823%252900395-1/fulltext", "title": "Intermittent Fasting: Exploring Approaches, Benefits, and ...", "summary": "Intermittent Fasting: Exploring Approaches, Benefits, and Implications for Health and Weight Management Article Info Publication History: Published online February 1, 2024 DOI: 10.1016/j.nurpra.2023.104893 External LinkAlso available on ScienceDirect External Link Copyright: \u00a9 2023 Elsevier Inc. Linked Articles (0) Download started OkKeywords Introduction Intermittent fasting (IF) is a popular weight loss method that has gained attention for its simplicity and results. Unlike traditional calorie-restricted diets, IF restricts the time when food is consumed.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Intermittent Fasting: Exploring Approaches, Benefits, and Implications for Health and Weight Management Article Info Publication History: Published online February 1, 2024 DOI: 10.1016/j.nurpra.2023.104893 External LinkAlso available on ScienceDirect", "Patients should consult a health care provider before beginning a new dietary regimen to review any ongoing medical condition(s) or if they are taking medication.", "According to the World Health Organization (WHO) classification, obesity is defined as follows: a BMI of 25 to 29.9 is considered overweight, a BMI of 30 to 39.5 is considered obese, and a BMI of 40 or greater is morbid obesity.1 1.", "Additionally, obesity can be a source of stress due to societal pressures and self-stigma.4 In 2019, the estimated annual medical expenditure for treating or managing obesity-related complications in the United States was nearly $173 billion.2 The me", "Centers for Disease Control and Prevention Adult obesity facts Published February 11, 2021 https://www.cdc.gov/obesity/data/adult.html 2."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Intermittent Fasting: Exploring Approaches, Benefits, and Implications for Health and Weight Management Article Info Pub"}, {"url": "https://www.hopkinsmedicine.org/health/expert-qa/intermittent-fasting-what-is-it-and-how-does-it-work", "title": "Intermittent Fasting: What Is It, And How Does It Work?", "summary": "Intermittent Fasting: What Is It, And How Does It Work? Updated: April 7, 2026 Intermittent fasting is an eating plan that switches between fasting and eating on a regular schedule. Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Updated: April 7, 2026 Intermittent fasting is an eating plan that switches between fasting and eating on a regular schedule.", "Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach.", "Key Points - Intermittent fasting focuses on when you eat instead of how much or what you eat. - Research shows that intermittent fasting is a way to manage your weight and prevent \u2014 or even reverse \u2014 some forms of disease. - There are several fastin", "With intermittent fasting, you only eat during a specific time of the day or on certain days of the week.", "Research shows fasting for a certain number of hours each day or eating just one meal a couple days a week may have health benefits."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Updated: April 7, 2026 Intermittent fasting is an eating plan that switches between fasting and eating on a regular sche"}]}
